title: Production, factory and assembly supervisors
description: Directly supervise and coordinate the activities of production and operating workers, such as inspectors, precision workers, machine setters and operators, assemblers, fabricators, and plant and system operators. Excludes team or work leaders. Assemble, fit, fasten, and install parts of airplanes, space vehicles, or missiles, such as tails, wings, fuselage, bulkheads, stabilizers, landing gear, rigging and control equipment, or heating and ventilating systems. Wind wire coils used in electrical components, such as resistors and transformers, and in electrical equipment and instruments, such as field cores, bobbins, armature cores, electrical motors, generators, and control equipment. (Based on 38 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Monitor recording instruments, flowmeters, panel lights, and other indicators, and listen for warning signals to verify conformity of process conditions.
Move control settings to make necessary adjustments on equipment units affecting speeds of chemical reactions, quality, or yields and respond to malfunctions by notifying appropriate personnel.
Inspect operating units, such as towers and tanks, to ensure functionality and efficiency, and conduct quality control tests to monitor processing and ensure standards are met.
Assemble prefabricated parts to form subassemblies, and attach components or subassemblies using bolts, screws, rivets, or other fasteners, following blueprints or specifications.
Inspect, operate, and test completed products to verify functionality and conformance to specifications, and adjust, repair, or replace parts or assemblies as necessary.
Perform equipment maintenance tasks, such as cleaning, oiling, and repairing machines and parts to ensure proper operation.
Select and measure or weigh ingredients, materials, and components according to specifications, ensuring correct quantities are allocated during production processes.
Operate or tend equipment to cook, mix, blend, or further process ingredients, following established procedures or recipes to maintain quality.
Cut, trim, bend, or smooth parts to ensure proper fit and clearance, using appropriate tools and machines as required.
Record production and test data, such as quantities processed and machine settings, to ensure accurate tracking of operations.
Operate wire-coiling machines to wind wire coils used in electrical components, cutting, stripping, and bending wire leads as required.

Record production and operational data on specified forms; record production data such as weight and amount of product processed, types of products, and time and temperature of processing; complete, review, or maintain production, time, or component waste reports.

Attach, alter, and trim materials such as wire, insulation, and coils, using hand tools; assemble parts or units, and position, align, and fasten units to assemblies, using hand tools and power tools; connect cables, tubes, and wiring, according to specifications.

Inspect products to ensure that specifications are met and to determine if machines need adjustment; monitor factors such as temperatures and dye flow rates to ensure they are within specified ranges; examine and feel products to identify defects and variations from standards; observe operations to detect defects, malfunctions, or supply shortages; observe and compare colors and consistencies of products to instrument readings and test results.

Start machines, monitor operation, and make adjustments as needed; stop machines to remove completed components; stop machines when specified amounts of products have been produced; operate machines for test runs to verify adjustments and to obtain product samples; notify supervisors or mechanics of equipment malfunctions; adjust equipment controls to maintain specified heat, tension, and speed; shut down machines when not in use.

Disassemble units to replace parts or to crate them for shipping; disassemble timepieces to make repairs; clean and lubricate timepiece parts and assemblies, using solvents, buff sticks, and oil; adjust, repair, or replace electrical or electronic components to correct defects; clean equipment with steam, hot water, and hoses; pull out defective material or remove blockages in bins or equipment using appropriate tools.

Confer with supervisors or engineers to plan or review work activities or resolve production problems; confer with coworkers to obtain information about orders, processing plans, or problems that occur; notify maintenance engineers of equipment malfunctions; review blueprints and specifications to determine materials requirements or assembly instructions.

Examine and adjust components for proper fit and assembly; inspect, test, and adjust completed units to ensure they meet specifications; measure parts to determine tolerances using precision measuring instruments; thread yarn, thread, and fabric through guides, needles, and rollers of machines for weaving, knitting, or other processing; install, level, and align components such as gears, chains, dies, cutters, and needles.

Bend parts, such as hairsprings and pallets, to correct deficiencies; install, level, and align machine components to set up machinery for operation; program electronic equipment; study guides and charts to determine setup requirements; operate small cranes to transport or position large parts; measure and adjust voltages to specified values to determine operational accuracy of instruments.

Examine, test, and record results of wired electrical components such as motors; disassemble and assemble motors; apply solutions or paints to wired electrical components; clean parts, using cleaning solutions, air hoses, and cloths; perform maintenance such as cleaning and oiling equipment and repairing or replacing worn parts.

Wind wire coils used in electrical components, such as resistors and transformers; perform tasks such as sewing ends together or removing dyed articles from machines; and pack or fold materials in preparation for shipment; perform miscellaneous tasks that support production processes.
Tend machines and equipment to ensure smooth operation and proper processing of materials; monitor machinery operations to ensure efficient production and coordination of tasks.
Attach hoses or nozzles to machines, using wrenches and pliers, and make adjustments to obtain the proper dispersion of spray.
Direct activities of workers assisting in control or verification of processes or in unloading of materials.

Weigh or measure chemicals, coatings, or paints before adding them to machines; record operational data on specified forms; weigh or measure specified amounts of ingredients or materials for processing using devices such as scales and calipers.

Turn dials, handwheels, valves, or switches to regulate conveyor speeds, machine temperature, air pressure and circulation, and the flow or spray of coatings or paints; start and stop operation of machines, using levers or buttons; adjust controls to regulate temperature, pressure, feed, or flow of liquids or gases and times of prescribed reactions according to knowledge of equipment and processes.

Fill hoppers, reservoirs, troughs, or pans with material used to coat, paint, or spray, using conveyors or pails; thread or feed items or products through or around machine rollers and dryers; operate auxiliary machines or equipment used in coating or painting processes.

Remove materials, parts, or workpieces from painting or coating machines, using hand tools; inspect coated or plated areas for defects, such as air bubbles or uneven coverage; examine, measure, weigh, or test sample products to ensure conformance to specifications.

Hold or position spray guns to direct spray onto articles; spray prepared surfaces with specified amounts of primers and decorative or finish coatings; apply rust-resistant undercoats and caulk and seal seams.

Draw samples of products at specified stages so that analyses can be performed; record operational data such as temperatures, pressures, ingredients used, processing times, or test results; collect samples of materials or products for laboratory analysis.

Determine paint flow, viscosity, and coating quality by performing visual inspections or by using viscometers; test product samples for specific gravity, chemical characteristics, pH levels, concentrations, or viscosities, and send them to laboratories for testing.

Patrol work areas to detect leaks or equipment malfunctions or to monitor operating conditions; observe machine gauges and equipment operation to detect defects or deviations from standards, and make adjustments as necessary.

Monitor painting operations to identify flaws such as blisters or streaks and correct their causes; inspect equipment or units to detect leaks or malfunctions, shutting equipment down if necessary; monitor gauges, recording instruments, flowmeters, or products to ensure that specified conditions are maintained.
Operate machines to process materials in compliance with applicable safety, energy, or environmental regulations; clean equipment and work areas; dispose of hazardous waste in an appropriate manner.

Disassemble, clean, and reassemble sprayers or power equipment, using solvents, wire brushes, and cloths; clean, lubricate, and adjust equipment using scrapers, solvents, air hoses, oil, and hand tools.

Transport materials and products to and from work areas manually or using carts, handtrucks, or hoists; load specified amounts of refined or unrefined materials into equipment or containers for further processing or storage.
Turn valves or move controls to admit, drain, separate, filter, clarify, mix, or transfer materials; press and adjust controls to activate, set, and regulate equipment according to specifications.

Record temperatures, amounts of materials processed, or test results on report forms; maintain logs of instrument readings, test results, or shift production for entry in computer databases; read and interpret work orders and instructions to determine work assignments, process specifications, and production schedules.
Set up, operate, or tend plating or coating machines to coat metal or plastic products with chromium, zinc, copper, cadmium, nickel, or other metal to protect or decorate surfaces.

Remove solidified steel or slag from pouring nozzles using long bars or oxygen burners; remove clogs, defects, or impurities from machines, tanks, conveyors, screens, or other processing equipment; skim slag or remove excess metal from ingots or equipment using hand tools, strainers, rakes, or burners, collecting scrap for recycling.
Record production data and maintain production logs; communicate processing instructions to other workers.

Inspect furnaces and equipment for defects and wear; examine molds to ensure they are clean, smooth, and properly coated; adjust dials to regulate flow of current and voltage supplied to terminals to control plating processes.

Read dials and gauges on panel control boards to ascertain temperatures, alkalinities, and densities of mixtures; inspect and flush lines with solutions or steam, and spray equipment with sterilizing solutions; monitor material flow or instruments, such as temperature or pressure gauges, indicators, or meters to ensure optimal processing conditions.

Operate lifting or moving devices to move equipment or materials to access areas to be painted; load equipment receptacles or conveyors with material to be processed, by hand or using hoists; assemble equipment and attach pipes, fittings, or valves using hand tools.

Collect samples, or signal workers to sample metal for analysis; draw smelted metal samples from furnaces or kettles for analysis and calculate types and amounts of materials needed to ensure that materials meet specifications; measure, weigh, and mix chemical ingredients according to specifications.
Inspect and flush lines with solutions or steam and spray equipment with sterilizing solutions.

Remove full containers from discharge outlets and replace them with empty containers; load and position wrapping paper, sticks, bags, or cartons into dispensing machines; inspect and flush lines with solutions or steam and spray equipment with sterilizing solutions; remove impurities from the surface of molten metal, using strainers.
Remove grease, dirt, paint, or rust from surfaces in preparation for paint application, using abrasives, solvents, brushes, blowtorches, washing tanks, or sandblasters.

Measure, mark, and mask areas to be excluded from plating; determine sizes and compositions of objects to be plated and amounts of electrical current and time required; monitor and measure thicknesses of electroplating on component parts to verify conformance to specifications, using micrometers.
Adjust machine or freezer speed and air intake to obtain desired consistency and amount of product.

Turn valves to pump sterilizing solutions or rinse water through pipes or equipment or to spray vats with atomizers; immerse workpieces in coating solutions or liquid metal or plastic for specified times; rinse coated objects in cleansing liquids and dry them with cloths, centrifugal driers, or by tumbling in sawdust-filled barrels.

Position objects to be plated in frames or suspend them from positive or negative terminals of power supplies; suspend sticks or pieces of plating metal from anodes or positive terminals, and immerse metal in plating solutions.
Test samples to determine viscosity, acidity, specific gravity, or degree of concentration, using test equipment such as viscometers, pH meters, or hydrometers.
Clean and maintain equipment, using water hoses and scrapers; inspect machines or equipment for hazards, operating efficiency, malfunctions, wear, or leaks.
Maintain production records and data, including meter readings, quantities, types, dimensions, and inspection or test results.
Monitor machine operations, observe gauges, and make necessary adjustments to ensure machines are operating properly and in conformance to specifications.
Adjust machine components and controls to regulate speeds, pressures, temperatures, and material flow during production processes.
Inspect, test, and verify compliance of materials, products, and installations, including measuring, weighing, and sorting items as required.
Operate or tend machines, perform maintenance tasks, and clean work areas to ensure safe and efficient production.
Remove defective products and conduct regular inspections and adjustments to ensure quality control.
Record and document production processes, work orders, and inspection results to ensure compliance and traceability.
Move materials, components, and finished products between work areas using hand tools and equipment.
Train and assess equipment operators and ensure they comply with safety and operational standards.
Perform equipment maintenance, including cleaning, lubrication, and minor repairs to maintain operational efficiency.
Install or adjust machinery and components according to specified requirements, using hand tools as necessary.
Collect samples for laboratory analysis, and manage quality assurance processes based on inspection results.
Handle, shape, and prepare materials for machine processing, ensuring proper dimensions and specifications.
Prepare and maintain necessary materials and equipment for production, including measuring ingredients and chemicals accurately.
Communicate production issues and collaborate with supervisors and other personnel to implement solutions.
Review specifications and instructions to determine proper procedures and settings for machinery operation.
Prepare components for etching, engraving, or finishing processes according to production requirements.
Analyze production processes to identify areas for improvement and implement necessary corrective actions as required.
Synchronize machine speeds and processes during multi-step production operations to maintain efficiency.
Remove molds and components post-production and ensure proper closure of processes using specified tools.

Direct and coordinate the activities of employees engaged in the production or processing of goods, such as inspectors, machine setters, or fabricators, including instructing new workers in machine operation. Monitor, evaluate, and enforce safety and sanitation regulations, and ensure compliance with production standards. Plan and establish work schedules, assignments, and production sequences to meet goals, and confer with management or subordinates to resolve worker problems.

Inspect, weigh, and measure products to verify conformance to specifications, using instruments such as micrometers, calipers, magnifiers, or rulers. Examine finished ware for defects and measure dimensions using rules and thickness gauges. Record manufacturing information, such as quantities, sizes, or types of goods produced and maintain supplies of tools, equipment, and materials.

Adjust and correct machine set-ups to reduce thicknesses, reshape products, and eliminate defects. Start operation of rolling and milling machines to flatten, temper, form, and reduce sheet metal sections and to produce steel strips. Change dies on extruding machines according to production line changes, and signal and assist other workers in these operations.

Read rolling orders, blueprints, and mill schedules to determine setup specifications, work sequences, product dimensions, and installation procedures. Measure and examine extruded products to locate defects and check for conformance to specifications, adjusting controls as necessary to alter products.

Operate and adjust controls of heating equipment to melt materials or to cure, dry, or bake filled molds. Align, assemble, or shape materials as required for production processes, using hand tools and gauges to verify dimensions and ensure quality.

Record mill production on schedule sheets, and keep records of employees' attendance and hours worked. Evaluate employee performance and recommend personnel actions such as hirings, evaluations, or promotions.

Maintain an inventory of materials, and troubleshoot, maintain, and make minor repairs to equipment as needed. Clean work areas and remove excess materials and level and smooth wet mold mixtures as part of the maintenance and production processes.

Examine parts to ensure metal shades and colors conform to specifications, using knowledge of metal heat-treating. Adjust controls to maintain temperatures and heating times, using thermal instruments and charts, as well as signal forklift operators for material handling, ensuring efficient production flow.
Teach and guide other workers in the operation of machinery or equipment, ensuring that production standards are maintained and that workers are trained in work and safety procedures.

Assemble, fit, fasten, and install parts as required, ensuring proper techniques are followed and quality standards achieved. This includes work on various materials such as metal, clay, and glass, using appropriate tools and equipment. Ensure the production of completed products meets design and functional specifications.
Interpret specifications, blueprints, job orders, and company policies and procedures for workers.
Maintain operations data, such as time, production, and cost records, and prepare management reports of production results.
Recommend or implement measures to motivate employees and improve production methods, equipment performance, product quality, or efficiency.
Determine standards, budgets, production goals, and rates based on company policies, equipment and labor availability, and workloads.
Requisition materials, supplies, equipment parts, or repair services.
Set up and adjust machines and equipment.
Inspect materials, products, or equipment to detect defects or malfunctions.
